Services can include case management; individual and family support plans; access to brokerage services for transportation; in-home supports or residential placement; and employment and vocational services. 4185 SW Research Way Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Individuals with an intellectual or developmental disability. Applicant must be an Oregon resident and a U.S. citizen. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (503) 842-8201 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a wide range of services that may include networking and outreach, facilitation of Individual Support Plan (ISP) meetings, assisting in the referral process to community service agencies for supports, providing support to individuals and families in a variety of planning meetings, assisting in finding residential placement and employment services, as well as referrals to support services brokerages. The DD program staff may also review complaints of abuse and immediately take steps to assess further risk. In some cases, protective services may be offered to avoid further harm. 817 SE 13th Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must have the all of the following: -A disability that originated in the brain -The disability must be established prior the age of 22, or in the case of intellectual disability the condition must be determined before 18 years of age. -Must result in significant impairments in at least two of the following areas of daily function: Self-care, communication, cognition, mobility, self direction, capacity of independent living, and/or economic self sufficiency -The impairments must not be primarily related to: substance abuse, mental illness, an emotional disorder, ADHD, a learning disability, or sensory impairment. Impairments must be expected to last indefinitely.
